items.claim: Aslan Bibayev relies entirely on his wrestling and lacks a viable striking game.
items.quote: he's live or die by the takedowns. He doesn't really seem to have a whole lot of striking in that back pocket.

items.claim: Aslan Bibayev lands fewer than 2.5 significant strikes per minute.
items.quote: He lands less than 2 and 1/2 significant strikes per minute

items.claim: Aslan Bibayev struggles to perform when his initial wrestling game plan is neutralized.
items.quote: When he couldn't take him down at all, you see that there really isn't a plan B, C, or D.

items.claim: Aslan Bibayev is ineffective when fighting off of his back.
items.quote: When Perez stuffed the takedown and ended up on top, this guy's not that great off of his back.

items.claim: Charles Johnson has significantly improved his takedown defense in recent fights.
items.quote: over the last number of fights, most of these guys aren't taking him down anymore and the guys that do take him down don't really just hold him down for long periods.

items.claim: Charles Johnson possesses a 5-inch reach advantage and a 5-inch height advantage over Aslan Bibayev.
items.quote: he's got a 5-in reach advantage over Asu and a 5-in height advantage over Asu.

items.claim: Charles Johnson is a superior striker capable of knocking out or outstriking top-tier division opponents.
items.quote: The guy's capable of of knocking out the best guys in the division. He's capable of outstriking the best guys in the division.
